_comment: Flavor text for Sigh's Hit Attribution module. Each top-level key is a layer type. Each layer has a damage-agnostic 'default' array plus one entry per damage type. A damage-type entry is either a flat array (range-agnostic) or an object with 'any' (range-agnostic), 'melee', and 'ranged' arrays — 'melee'/'ranged' are preferred when the attack's range is known, falling back to 'any', then to the layer's 'default'. One line is chosen at random from the resolved pool. Tokens: {attacker} {attack} {defender} {armorName} {shieldName} {spellName} {dexMod} {abilityMod} {abilityAbbr} {vs} {ves} {isAre} {wasWere}

default: {attacker}'s {attack} go{vs} hopelessly wide — {isAre} never going to land.
{attacker}'s {attack} miss{ves} completely, connecting with nothing but air.
A clumsy {attack} from {attacker} find{vs} nothing but empty space.
{attacker} overextend{vs} on the {attack} and nearly loses their footing entirely.
{attacker} commits fully to the {attack} and finds only empty space for the effort.
The {attack} never had a real angle on {defender} to begin with.
{attacker}'s footing falters mid-swing — the {attack} {wasWere} doomed from the start.
Not even close. {defender} didn't need to move a muscle.
